name: Confidence-Weighted BM25 (CWBM25)
overview: TrustDB implements BM25 full-text search with confidence weighting directly in the storage engine. Document scores are modulated by the confidence envelope of each section, so high-confidence documents rank higher than low-confidence ones at equal textual relevance. This runs inline with storage reads — no separate search index service required.

howItWorks.title: Inverted Index
howItWorks.description: A compact inverted index is maintained alongside the B-epsilon tree. Tokens are mapped to posting lists containing document IDs, term frequencies, and references to the source LeafEntry. The index is updated during B-epsilon flush operations.

howItWorks.title: BM25 Scoring
howItWorks.description: Standard BM25 scoring computes term frequency, inverse document frequency, and field length normalization. Parameters k1=1.2 and b=0.75 are tuned for the typical section-length distribution.

howItWorks.title: Confidence Weighting
howItWorks.description: The raw BM25 score is multiplied by the section's current confidence (after TDQP decay). This means a highly relevant but low-confidence document ranks below a moderately relevant high-confidence one.

howItWorks.title: Inline Execution
howItWorks.description: Search executes within the storage engine process — no serialization, network calls, or index synchronization. The inverted index shares the same MVCC snapshot as the data read.

comparisons.source: Optimized for search; faster at pure text retrieval
Purpose-built search engine; significantly faster at scale
Rust-native search library; faster but no confidence integration

comparisons.narrative: TrustDB's full-text search is deliberately not trying to beat Elasticsearch at pure search throughput — at 1K documents, Elasticsearch is roughly 14x faster. The value proposition is different: CWBM25 provides confidence-weighted ranking with zero index synchronization lag, all within a single storage engine. For use cases where confidence-aware search matters more than raw query throughput, this eliminates the operational complexity of running a separate search cluster.

competitors.approach: Distributed inverted index with BM25 and custom scoring
competitors.limitation: Separate service to operate; index sync lag; no native confidence weighting (requires custom script_score)
Library, not integrated storage; no confidence envelopes; application must manage index lifecycle
No BM25 (uses ts_rank); no confidence weighting; separate index maintenance

competitors.approach: Typo-tolerant search optimized for instant results
competitors.limitation: Separate service; no confidence semantics; designed for user-facing search, not data retrieval
